root_cause.text: The `options` string is not freed before throwing a reader exception when the number of meta channels exceeds `MaxPixelChannels`, leading to a memory leak.
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds a call to `DestroyString(options);` immediately before `ThrowReaderException` inside the check for `image->number_meta_channels > MaxPixelChannels` in `coders/mpc.c`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `options` string is not freed before throwing a reader exception when the number of meta channels exceeds `MaxPixelChannels`, leading to a memory leak.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds a call to `DestroyString(options);` immediately before `ThrowReaderException` inside the check for `image->number_meta_channels > MaxPixelChannels` in `coders/mpc.c`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix a memory leak by destroying the options string before throwing a reader exception.
intent_record.what: Added a call to DestroyString(options) right before throwing an exception for improper image headers.
intent_record.how: Frees the dynamically allocated 'options' string before the function exits via the exception macro, preventing a memory leak on the error path.

grouping_result.intent: Fix a memory leak by destroying the options string before throwing a reader exception.

relevance_review.reason: The group's intent directly addresses the memory leak described in the root cause by freeing the options string before throwing the exception.

candidates.rationale: This line throws the reader exception without first freeing the 'options' string, leading to the memory leak.
This condition triggers the path that throws the exception and leaks the 'options' string.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement throws the reader exception without freeing the 'options' string, leading to the memory leak.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Added check for incorrect number of meta channels report in #492.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 7fd419441bc7103398e313558171d342c6315f44
Author: Dirk Lemstra <dirk@git.imagemagick.org>
Date:   Mon May 15 21:43:29 2017 +0200

    Added check for incorrect number of meta channels report in #492.

diff --git a/coders/mpc.c b/coders/mpc.c
index 8088da492..86359572d 100644
--- a/coders/mpc.c
+++ b/coders/mpc.c
@@ -568,6 +568,9 @@ static Image *ReadMPCImage(const ImageInfo *image_info,ExceptionInfo *exception)
                 if (LocaleCompare(keyword,"number-meta-channels") == 0)
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces a check for 'number_meta_channels > MaxPixelChannels' which calls 'ThrowReaderException(CorruptImageError, "ImproperImageHeader")'. However, it fails to free the 'options' string before throwing the exception, which introduces the memory leak described in the vulnerability root cause.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b6781acb6da0bfccd07e5f91f5793cdfc38672a0
Author: Cristy <urban-warrior@imagemagick.org>
Date:   Sat Jan 28 18:41:37 2017 -0500

    ...

diff --git a/coders/mpc.c b/coders/mpc.c
index 6f891acf2..8a2a3cdc0 100644
--- a/coders/mpc.c
+++ b/coders/mpc.c
@@ -557,6 +557,21 @@ static Image *ReadMPCImage(const ImageInfo *image_info,ExceptionInfo *exception)
                 (void) SetImageProperty(image,keyword,options,exception);
                 break;
               }
+              case 'n':
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the parsing of 'number-meta-channels', but does not contain the logic that throws a reader exception when it exceeds MaxPixelChannels. Since the described vulnerable construct (the exception throw leaking the options string) is absent from this snippet, it does not contain the bug.
